title: Scaled Dot-Product Attention
audience: ML students who know vectors and softmax but want a concrete attention mechanism walk-through.
central_question: How do query-key scores become normalized weights that mix value vectors?

teaching_units.name: Q, K, and V Roles
teaching_units.goal: Separate what the query asks for, what keys advertise, and what values carry forward.
teaching_units.visual_example: Three labeled columns show query, keys, and values for the same token sequence, with distinct colors.

teaching_units.name: Scaled Dot-Product Scores
teaching_units.goal: Show how query-key similarity produces one raw score per context token and why sqrt(d_k) scaling matters.
teaching_units.visual_example: A query vector dots with several key vectors; raw score bars shrink after scaling.

teaching_units.name: Softmax Attention Weights
teaching_units.goal: Animate raw scores into a probability distribution over context positions.
teaching_units.visual_example: Score bars morph into normalized weights that sum to one, with the largest relevant token highlighted.

teaching_units.name: Weighted Value Mixture
teaching_units.goal: Explain that the attention output is a weighted blend of value vectors.
teaching_units.visual_example: Weighted arrows from value cards flow into one output card, with thicker arrows for larger weights.

comparison_axes: raw dot products vs normalized softmax weights
formula presentation vs geometric vector intuition
